title: Elsholtzia lavandulispica

Elsholtzia lavandulispica is often referred to as a lavender-flowered Elsholtzia due to its distinctive purple flowers. The species is also known to be a member of the mint family, which comprises many other herbs and ornamental plants. Elsholtzia lavandulispica is a perennial plant that grows in a variety of habitats, including dry scrublands and grasslands.
